premise: My mother tricked me into signing a power of attorney while I was deployed overseas, sold the $190,000 house my grandmother left me, and kept every cent. A decade later she suffered a stroke and begged to move in — I told her I’d bought the house back at foreclosure auction for $85,000 and it was now occupied by a family who actually deserved it.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Mom Tricked Me, Sold My Inheritance While I Was Deployed. So I Bought It Back
